narrative.description: Extract explicit factual points from narrative text (novels or screenplays). Focus on what the text clearly states.
guidelines: Capture concrete facts: actions taken, states, events, decisions, outcomes.
Include important named entities and what the text explicitly says about them.
Include salient events that change the situation or state.
Include stable or clearly stated relationships if present.
Include document-specific terms or names if they carry meaning in this text.
Do NOT infer motivations, themes, symbolism, or future developments.

general.description: Extract explicit factual points from general documents (papers, technical docs, reports) for retrieval and QA.
guidelines: Capture concrete statements: definitions, claims, constraints, assumptions, comparisons.
Capture methods, components, or procedures explicitly described.
Capture results, findings, or conclusions if stated.
Capture limitations or caveats only if explicitly mentioned.
Include document-specific terms, systems, datasets, or abbreviations.
Do NOT summarize broadly or add interpretation.
